evidence: The highest scores reflect the physical (ontological.material), destructive (symmetry.destructive), and existential (resonance.existential) nature of decay. Breaking down touches the core essence (resonance.essential) of deterioration, operates predictably (lawfulness.predictable), and can involve ethical implications (value_generation.ethical) when neglect causes decay.

The phrase 'break down' strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical decomposition) and symmetry.destructive (pathological decay). It also connects to temporal.transformation (fundamental change) and resonance.essential (core essence of deterioration). Lower scores reflect direct physical manifestation and limited ethical/aesthetic implications.

Break up strongly resonates with material decomposition (ontological.material), fundamental transformation (temporal.transformation), and destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive). It also connects to perception (epistemological.perception) and lawful inevitability (lawfulness.necessary).

Breakable strongly resonates with decay's essence (resonance.essential) and material fragility (ontological.material). Its decay-driven transformation (temporal.transformation) and predictable deterioration (lawfulness.predictable) align with CHG.decay. Interconnection.chained reflects cascading breakdowns, while symmetry.destructive captures its dark, decay-oriented nature.

The verb 'bust' in the context of decay strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical deterioration), symmetry.destructive (direct opposition to growth), and resonance.essential (core transformation of existence). It also weakly connects to scale.individual (single entities decaying), epistemological.perception/identification (direct sensory naming of decay), and lawfulness.predictable (patterns in decay processes).

Corrode strongly resonates with material decay (ontological.material 10), ongoing temporal processes (temporal.influence 7, temporal.continuation 8), and destructive dynamics (symmetry.destructive 10). It is perceived through sensory observation (epistemological.perception 8) and follows predictable chemical patterns (lawfulness.predictable 8). Its essential nature touches the core of decline (resonance.essential 10).

Corroding strongly resonates with temporal.influence (ongoing deterioration) and ontological.material (physical decay). It aligns with symmetry.destructive (pathological breakdown) and lawfulness.predictable (following known patterns like rust). Scores drop elsewhere due to lack of direct connection to other slots.

Corrosion strongly resonates with temporal.influence (active deterioration over time), ontological.material (physical process), and symmetry.destructive (its inherently destructive nature). Scores for scale.individual and ontological.structural reflect its localized and structural impact. Lower scores reflect its lack of connection to abstract or systemic value, ethical dimensions, or existential resonance.

Corrosive strongly resonates with destructive processes (symmetry.destructive), ongoing temporal influence (temporal.influence), and material decay (ontological.material). Its essential resonance with the core of CHG.decay (resonance.essential) and structural patterns of decline (ontological.structural) further highlight its alignment with the atom's definition.

Corruption strongly resonates with societal decay (scale.society 10), destructive dynamics (symmetry.destructive 10), ethical implications (value.ethical 9), and material/structural deterioration (ontological.material 8, ontological.structural 9). Its influence over time (temporal.influence 7) and catalytic spread (interconnection.catalytic 6) are central to its meaning. Lower scores reflect its lack of cosmic scale or existential resonance.

The verb 'crack' strongly resonates with decay's destructive process (symmetry.destructive 10), material deterioration (ontological.material 10), and catalyzing further breakdown (interconnection.catalytic 7). It signals early signs (temporal.indication 5) and active influence over decline (temporal.influence 7). Perception (8) and identification (7) are key, as cracks are visible markers of decay. Structural resonance (ontological.structural 5) ties to how cracks disrupt organizational integrity.

Cracking resonates strongly with destructive processes (symmetry.destructive 7) and material/structural breakdown (ontological.material 6, ontological.structural 4). It indicates decay's onset (temporal.indication 3) and influences its progression (temporal.influence 4). Its superficial appearance (resonance.superficial 3) contrasts with structural transformation (resonance.structural 4). Predictable in material decay (lawfulness.predictable 4), but contingent on conditions (lawfulness.contingent 3). Ethical implications arise from neglect (value_generation.ethical 3), while functional value relates to structural integrity (value_generation.functional 3).

Crumble strongly resonates with ontological.material (10) as it describes physical breakdown, symmetry.destructive (9) due to its inherent decay, and temporal.transformation (8) as it signifies fundamental change. Scores in temporal.influence (7) and interconnection.chained (6) reflect its ongoing and cascading effects.

Decadent as a noun in the context of decay strongly resonates with temporal aspects (ongoing influence, transformation, persistence), material/structural ontological layers, and destructive symmetry. It also connects to ethical value (moral decline), predictable lawfulness, and essential resonance as decay touches the core essence of a thing. Lower scores reflect its focus on physical decline rather than abstract scales or systemic interconnections.

Decay strongly resonates with temporal processes (influence, transformation, continuation), material/structural decline, societal/moral decay (ethical), and its opposition to growth (symmetry.destructive). Scores are low for non-temporal scales and abstract categories.

Decay strongly resonates with temporal aspects like ongoing influence and transformation over time. It operates on material and structural levels, is inherently destructive in symmetry, and touches the essential core of decline. Predictable in many contexts, it also carries ethical implications (e.g., neglect).

Decline strongly resonates with temporal aspects (ongoing influence, transformation over time), material/structural ontological layers (physical deterioration), and symmetry.destructive (as the inverse of growth). Scores peak at resonance.essential (core essence of decay) and symmetry.destructive (pathological opposition to growth).

Decompose strongly resonates with temporal processes (influence, transformation) and material/ecomaterial scales. Its destructive symmetry and essential resonance reflect decay's core essence. Scores drop where connections are indirect or speculative.

Decomposition strongly resonates with temporal.influence (active process over time), symmetry.destructive (core to decay's opposition to growth), and ontological.material (physical breakdown). It also aligns with catalytic interconnections (triggering new growth) and functional value (e.g., soil enrichment).

Deteriorate strongly resonates with temporal.influence (7) and temporal.continuation (8) as it inherently involves ongoing decline. It scores highly in ontological.material (7) and ontological.structural (4) due to its physical and organizational breakdown. Resonance.essential (7) reflects its core impact on the entity's nature, while symmetry.destructive (10) opposes growth. Lawfulness.predictable (7) highlights its patterned nature.

Deterioration strongly resonates with temporal.influence (ongoing effect over time), ontological.material (physical decay), resonance.essential (core essence of decline), symmetry.destructive (opposite of growth), and value_generation.ethical (moral decay). Scores reflect its direct sensory perception, structural breakdown, and predictable yet fundamental nature.

Digest strongly resonates with material processes (ontological.material), essential resonance (core breakdown), destructive symmetry (breaking down), predictable bodily function (lawfulness.predictable), and functional utility (sustaining life). Scores reflect its role in physical decomposition over time, contrasting with growth's symmetry.

Digestion strongly resonates with transformation (5) and material (8) as it fundamentally breaks down matter. It aligns with decay's temporal influence (2) and lawful predictability (7). Scores drop where growth (CHG.grow's symmetric pair) or non-physical scales (cosmic) are implied.

Dilapidate strongly resonates with temporal processes (influence, transformation, continuation) and material/structural decay. Its destructive symmetry and contingent predictability align with CHG.decay's essence. Scores drop where the word opposes growth (symmetric pair) or lacks direct connection to abstract layers like cosmic or ethical.

Disintegrate strongly resonates with destructive symmetry (directly tied to decay), material ontology (physical breakdown), and essential resonance (core essence breakdown). Moderate scores reflect its visible nature (perception), inevitability in some contexts (necessary lawfulness), and ethical implications (moral decay).

Disintegration strongly resonates with temporal transformation (fundamental breakdown), ontological material (physical decay), and symmetry destructive (core of decay). It moderately connects to scale.individual/ecosystem (scope of decay), interconnection.chained (cause-effect breakdowns), and ethical implications (moral dimensions of decline).

Dissociate strongly resonates with temporal.influence (10) as it actively drives decay over time. Symmetry.destructive (10) reflects its role in breaking down, opposing growth. Resonance.essential (10) underscores its core role in the decay process. Ontological.material (10) highlights its physical manifestation in decomposition.

Dissociation strongly resonates with destructive symmetry (core to decay's destructive nature), material ontology (physical decomposition), and essential resonance (touching the core of decay). It also aligns with temporal influence (ongoing process) and chained interconnection (cause-effect in decay).

Droop strongly resonates with temporal.indication (as an early sign of decline), scale.individual (physical sagging of a single entity), and symmetry.destructive (its association with decay's negative, deteriorating aspect). It also aligns with ontological.material (physical manifestation), epistemological.perception (directly observable), and resonance.essential (core indicator of decay). Scores are low elsewhere due to lack of direct connection to broader systemic, abstract, or value-based dimensions.

Droop strongly resonates with material decay (ontological.material 10) and its visible, sensory manifestation (epistemological.perception 7). It reflects ongoing temporal influence (5) and aligns with destructive symmetry (7), fitting CHG.decay's deterioration theme. Lower scores reflect its physical, individual-scale focus and lack of broader systemic or value implications.

The phrase 'eat at' strongly resonates with temporal.influence (ongoing deterioration), symmetry.destructive (directly embodies decay), and ontological.material (physical breakdown). Moderate scores reflect its role in catalyzing change (interconnection.chained), ethical implications (value_generation.ethical), and structural resonance (resonance.structural).

Erode strongly resonates with temporal.influence (ongoing deterioration), ontological.material (physical breakdown), and symmetry.destructive (direct opposite of growth). Moderate scores reflect its scale (individual/ecosystem effects), structural transformation, and predictable patterns.

Erosion strongly resonates with decay's physical, time-based nature. It operates on material structures (ontological.material), actively influences change over time (temporal.influence), and causes fundamental transformation (temporal.transformation). Its destructive essence aligns with symmetry.destructive, while its predictable patterns (lawfulness.predictable) and structural effects (resonance.structural) further highlight its core role in decline.

Erosive strongly resonates with destructive processes (symmetry.destructive), physical matter (ontological.material), and predictable decline (lawfulness.predictable). Secondary scores reflect its structural impact (ontological.structural) and operation at individual/ecosystem scales.

Exponential decay strongly resonates with temporal slots (influence, transformation, continuation) as it describes a fundamental, ongoing process of decline. Its structural pattern aligns with ontological.structural and resonance.structural/essential. As the inverse of CHG.grow, symmetry.destructive is key. Lawfulness.predictable and necessary reflect its mathematical inevitability. Deep comprehension and functional utility highlight its epistemological and value roles.

The term resonates strongly with destructive symmetry (5) as it embodies accelerating decline. Its predictable mathematical nature scores high (5) and its structural resonance (4) reflects the exponential pattern's core role. Functional value (2) arises from its utility in modeling decay processes.

The phrase strongly resonates with temporal processes (ongoing decay, transformation), material/structural breakdown, and relational/social collapse. Destructive symmetry and necessary lawfulness reflect inevitability. Ethical implications arise when decay stems from neglect or failure to maintain.

Gnaw resonates strongly with temporal processes (influence, continuation) and material ontology. Its destructive symmetry and essential role in decay are central. It operates on an individual scale and can catalyze further decay through cause-effect chains.

The phrase 'gnaw at' strongly resonates with temporal.influence and temporal.continuation due to its connotation of persistent, ongoing deterioration. It aligns with symmetry.destructive as the direct opposite of growth. Scores for ontological.material and resonance.essential reflect its core role in physical/structural decay. Lower scores reflect tangential connections like scale.individual or ethical implications in specific contexts.

Putrefaction strongly resonates with temporal.influence (ongoing decay) and ontological.material (physical rot). It scores highly in symmetry.destructive (core pathology of decay) and epistemological.perception (direct sensory evidence). Lower scores reflect its localized scale and lack of systemic value generation.

Putrefy strongly resonates with temporal.influence (ongoing decay), symmetry.destructive (its inherently destructive nature), lawfulness.predictable (natural process), ontological.material (physical decomposition), and resonance.essential (core essence of decay). Scores reflect its direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception), individual-scale operation, and semantic alignment with CHG.decay.

Putrescence strongly resonates with temporal.influence (ongoing decay), temporal.transformation (fundamental rot), and ontological.material (physical decay). It connects to scale.individual/ecosystem (decomposition at both levels) and symmetry.destructive (its inherent decay nature). Epistemological perception/experience reflect direct sensory engagement with decay.

Putrescent strongly resonates with decay's temporal progression (transformation, influence), material decay (ontological.material), and destructive symmetry. Scores peak where decay's essence (resonance.essential) and physical manifestation (ontological.material) align. Lower scores reflect its lack of ethical/aesthetic value or systemic scale beyond individual entities.

Putrid strongly resonates with temporal aspects of decay's influence and transformation, material ontology, sensory perception, and destructive symmetry. Scores reflect its direct link to physical decay's progression and negative valuation.

Putridness strongly resonates with material decay (ontological.material 10), destructive processes (symmetry.destructive 10), and sensory perception (epistemological.perception 8). It marks fundamental transformation (temporal.transformation 7) and is a predictable sign of decline (lawfulness.predictable 7). Scores are low elsewhere as it lacks broader systemic or existential implications.

Rot strongly resonates with temporal processes like ongoing influence and fundamental transformation, operates at individual and ecosystem scales, is perceptible and tied to material/structural decay. Its essential and existential resonance reflects its core role in decline, while destructive symmetry and predictable lawfulness align with its nature as CHG.decay.

Rot fundamentally operates on the material layer (ontological.material), is a destructive process (symmetry.destructive), exerts influence over time (temporal.influence), and represents a predictable decline (lawfulness.predictable). These slots capture its core essence and observable patterns.

Rotten strongly resonates with material decay (ontological.material), transformation (temporal.transformation), and destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive). It applies to individual/societal scales and relies on sensory perception (epistemological.perception). Ethical resonance occurs in metaphorical contexts (e.g., 'rotten leadership').

Rottenness strongly resonates with material decay (ontological.material), temporal transformation (temporal.transformation), and destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive). It is perceptible (epistemological.perception) and operates at an individual scale (scale.individual), reflecting CHG.decay's focus on deterioration over time.

Rotting strongly resonates with temporal transformation (8) and influence (7) as it fundamentally changes an object over time. Its material nature (10) and destructive symmetry (10) reflect its physical decay. Essential resonance (7) and transformative symmetry (8) highlight its core essence and metamorphic process.

Ruin strongly resonates with material decay (10) and fundamental transformation (10). It opposes growth (symmetry.destructive 9) and is often seen as inevitable (lawfulness.necessary 7). Perception (8) and aesthetic value (6) highlight its tangible, observable nature. Structural breakdown (7) and existential resonance (5) reflect its core essence.

Ruin strongly resonates with temporal transformation (fundamental decay), ontological material (physical deterioration), and symmetry.destructive (direct opposition to growth). Moderate scores reflect its scale across individuals/societies, experiential perception, and aesthetic/ethical undertones.

Rust strongly resonates with ontological.material (physical decay), symmetry.destructive (its corrosive nature), and resonance.essential (core to the decay process). It weakly connects to temporal aspects (emergence, influence) and epistemological感知 (visibility). Most slots score 0 as rust's meaning is tightly bound to physical deterioration without broader systemic or existential implications.

Rust strongly resonates with temporal.influence (ongoing deterioration), temporal.continuation (persistence over time), symmetry.destructive (core destructive process), ontological.material (physical decay), and resonance.essential (touches the object's core essence). Lower scores reflect its visible nature (perception), dependency on conditions (contingent), and early signs (indication).

The word 'wear' as a noun strongly resonates with temporal aspects like ongoing influence and continuation (e.g., 'wear and tear'), material decay, and superficial physical deterioration. It aligns with destructive symmetry (opposite of growth) and predictable patterns of decline. Perception and identification scores reflect direct sensory observation of decay.

Wear strongly resonates with CHG.decay's destructive nature (symmetry.destructive 8) and material deterioration (ontological.material 7). It emphasizes ongoing temporal influence (temporal.influence 7) and persistence (temporal.continuation 5). The process aligns with structural patterns (resonance.structural 3) and is tied to physical observation (epistemological.experience 3).

The phrase 'wear away' strongly resonates with temporal processes (influence, continuation), material decay (ontological.material), and destructive symmetry. It is tied to direct observation (perception/experience) and lawful predictability. Lower scores reflect its focus on gradual loss rather than systemic transformation or existential depth.

The phrase 'wear out' strongly resonates with temporal aspects like ongoing influence (7) and fundamental transformation (8), as well as ontological material decay (10). It aligns with symmetry's destructive nature (10) and the predictable decline (8) inherent in CHG.decay. Functional value loss (5) and structural degradation (2) further reinforce its connection to deterioration over time.

The phrase 'wearing away' strongly resonates with temporal processes (influence, transformation, continuation) and material decay (ontological.material). It aligns with destructive symmetry and predictable lawfulness. Lower scores reflect its lack of connection to growth-related concepts (symmetry.generative, scale.cosmic) or abstract value layers.

Weather's temporal influence (e.g., erosion over time) and catalytic role in decay (e.g., rain causing rot) strongly resonate. Its material and structural presence (e.g., wind, water) directly drive decay processes, while its essential and destructive qualities align with CHG.decay's definition.

The verb 'weather' in the context of CHG.decay strongly resonates with the material layer (10) and destructive symmetry (10), as it directly involves physical deterioration. Scores are high for transformation (7) and essential resonance (7), reflecting decay's core nature. Moderate scores reflect its influence over time (5), predictable patterns (5), and contingent conditions (3).

Wilt strongly resonates with physical deterioration (ontological.material), destructive processes (symmetry.destructive), fundamental transformation (temporal.transformation), and core essence of decay (resonance.essential). Scores reflect its tangible, individual-scale manifestation and predictable decline patterns.

Wilt strongly resonates with temporal slots (indication, influence, transformation) as it marks stages of decay. It operates at the individual scale (e.g., a plant) and material layer. Symmetry.destructive aligns with its opposition to growth. Lawfulness.predictable reflects its expected nature in natural processes.

Wilting strongly resonates with physical (ontological.material), individual scale (scale.individual), and destructive symmetry (symmetry.destructive). It is visually perceptible (epistemological.perception), predictably tied to conditions like drought (lawfulness.predictable), and operates independently (interconnection.independent). Scores reflect its surface-level (resonance.superficial) and time-based decline (temporal.influence).
